Plaintiffs' Motion For An Order Permitting Amended/Supplemental Pared Down List And Deeming James Srebro, M.D. Timely Designated
Off Calendar Jan-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Carole M. Bosch, Esq. of PAUL and HANLEY (510-559-9980), appearing via Court Call for plaintiff / moving party Natalie Lucas
No other appearances.

RULING:
Plaintiffs Natalie Lucas, et al.'s application permitting an amended/supplemental pared down expert list and deeming James Srebro, M.D. timely designated. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion is granted. Plaintiffs have shown that their failure to timely disclose expert Dr. Srebro, a cardiologist, was due to mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Plaintiffs have also shown good cause for their application due to the fact that decedent's cause of death is a disputed issue. Defendant Certainteed has not shown that it will be prejudiced because defendants have retained Dr. Breall, another cardiologist, who will testify on defendants' behalf. Although Dr. Breall was retained by Berry & Berry rather than Certainteed, Certainteed has not established that Dr. Breall cannot adequately protect its interests or that any cardiologist retained by it would provide different opinions from those of Dr. Breall's. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported.
